Yes — in 2022 3M purchased $1,749 million of property, plant and equipment, had net property, plant and equipment of $9,178 million, and recorded depreciation and amortization of $1,831 million, indicating a capital-intensive business.

Operating margin change in FY2022 was driven primarily by organic growth/productivity and other (+1.0 percentage point, including benefits from strong pricing, spending discipline and prior restructuring) which was more than offset by raw material and logistics inflation (-2.4 percentage points), with additional negative impacts from foreign exchange, divestitures, higher other expense (including increased non-service pension/postretirement costs), and net costs for significant litigation and PFAS- and Russia-exit related charges. 3M reports operating income margin on both a reported and an adjusted (excluding special items) basis and discusses these components in its MD&A.
Consumer segment.

The quick ratio for Q2 FY2023 = (Cash $4,258 + Marketable securities $56 + Accounts receivable $4,947) / Current liabilities $10,936 ≈ 0.85. Although below 1, 3M states it "maintains a strong liquidity profile" supported by $4.3 billion of cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, a $4.25 billion revolving credit facility (undrawn at June 30, 2023) and access to commercial paper ( $1.8 billion outstanding), so the quick ratio alone may not fully reflect the Company's liquidity.
None of 3M’s debt securities are registered to trade on a national securities exchange under 3M’s name as of Q2 2023.

Yes — cash dividends declared and paid totaled $1.50 and $1.49 per share for the first and second quarters of 2023 and 2022, respectively, or $3.00 and $2.98 per share for the first six months of 2023 and 2022, respectively.

No — Adobe’s operating margin declined year-over-year to 34.7% in FY2022 (operating income $6,098m on revenue $17,606m) from 36.8% in FY2021 (operating income $5,802m on revenue $15,785m), though it was higher than FY2020’s 32.9%. Operating margin trends can be influenced by the company’s subscription revenue recognition (revenue is generally recognized ratably over subscription terms) and substantial ongoing investments in research and development, sales and marketing and acquisitions that affect operating expenses, so short-term operating margin movements may be less indicative of underlying business performance.

Yes — free cash flow conversion (operating cash flow minus purchases of property and equipment, divided by net income) rose to about 156% in FY2022 ($7,838M − $442M = $7,396M FCF; net income $4,756M) from about 143% in FY2021 and about 101% in FY2020.

Inventory turnover = $10,069 million ÷ (($1,055 + $604)/2 million) ≈ 12.1 times, so AES sold its inventory roughly 12 times in FY2022. Conventional inventory management is meaningful because the Company reports inventory as fuel and other raw materials ($733 million) and spare parts and supplies ($322 million).

The substitution of Amcor Flexibles North America, Inc. for Amcor Finance (USA), Inc. as issuer and the assumption by the substitute issuer of the former issuer’s covenants under the indentures governing the 3.625% 2026 and 4.500% 2028 guaranteed senior notes.
AMCOR's quick ratio improved from approximately 0.67 in FY2022 to approximately 0.69 in FY2023.

FY2023 — Acquired 100% equity interest in a Czech Republic flexible packaging manufacturing plant on August 1, 2022 for $59 million; a medical device packaging manufacturing site in Shanghai on March 17, 2023 for $60 million (including $20 million contingent consideration); and a New Zealand automated protein packaging machines manufacturer on May 31, 2023 for $45 million (including $13 million contingent consideration). FY2022 — No acquisitions reported; the Company completed the disposal of non‑core assets in the Flexibles reporting segment. FY2021 — No acquisitions reported; the Company completed the disposal of a non‑core European hospital supplies business, disposed of two non‑core businesses in India and Argentina, and sold its equity investment in AMVIG Holdings Limited.
The packaging industry.

No — consolidated gross profit as a percentage of net sales decreased to 18.5% in fiscal year 2023 from 19.4% in fiscal year 2022, so gross margin did not improve. The decrease was mainly from the pass-through of higher raw material costs and the impact of disposed operations, although excluding currency and disposed operations gross profit rose reflecting favorable operating cost performance.

AMCOR's restructuring liability at December 31, 2022 consists of accruals for employee costs, fixed-asset related costs and other costs arising from restructuring activities (primarily related to the Russia–Ukraine conflict and the prior Bemis integration), recorded on the unaudited condensed consolidated balance sheets under other current liabilities and other non‑current liabilities, with the company expecting the majority of the liability to be paid within the next twelve months.

Yes — AMD’s quick ratio for FY22 is approximately (4,835 + 1,020 + 4,126 + 2) / 6,369 ≈ 1.57, indicating a reasonably healthy liquidity profile because quick assets exceed current liabilities.

AMD's major products and services include server microprocessors (CPUs), graphics processing units (GPUs), data processing units (DPUs), field programmable gate arrays (FPGAs), and Adaptive System-on-Chip (SoC) products for data centers; CPUs, accelerated processing units (APUs) that integrate CPUs and GPUs, and chipsets for desktop and notebook PCs; discrete GPUs, semi-custom SoC products and development services; embedded CPUs, GPUs, APUs, FPGAs and Adaptive SoC products; and occasional sales or licensing of portions of its intellectual property portfolio.

Revenue changes in 2022 were driven by a 64% increase in Data Center sales primarily from higher EPYC server processor sales; a 10% decline in Client revenue due to a 24% decrease in unit shipments partially offset by a 19% increase in average selling price from a richer mix of Ryzen mobile processors; a 21% increase in Gaming revenue driven by higher semi‑custom gaming console SoC sales partially offset by lower gaming graphics unit shipments; and a significant Embedded increase primarily from the inclusion of Xilinx embedded product revenue following the February 2022 acquisition.

The change in operating margin for FY22 was primarily driven by amortization of intangible assets associated with the Xilinx acquisition, which reduced gross margin and caused operating income to decline versus 2021.
Operating activities: net cash provided of $3,565 million in FY22.

No — pretax margin (pretax income divided by total revenues net of interest expense) declined from 25.2% in 2021 to 18.1% in 2022. It did, however, improve versus 2020, rising from 11.9% to 18.1% in 2022.

Gross margin is not a useful metric for American Express because it is a financial services company that reports "Total revenues net of interest expense" and recognizes revenue as discount revenue, net card fees, service fees, processed revenue and interest income rather than product-level gross profit. Changes in underlying profitability in 2022 were driven by movements in discount revenue (merchant discount rates, acceptance and volumes), net interest income and interest expense (funding costs and loan yields), and by provisions for credit losses, card member rewards, business development and other operating expenses.
It decreased by 3.0 percentage points, from 24.6% in 2021 to 21.6% in 2022.

Yes — during FY2022 Boeing reported multiple ongoing legal actions related to the October 29, 2018 Lion Air Flight 610 and March 10, 2019 Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302 accidents and an arbitration with Embraer over terminated joint venture agreements.

Yes — Boeing’s consolidated gross margin improved to approximately 5.3% in 2022 (gross profit $3,502M on revenues $66,608M) from approximately 4.8% in 2021 ($3,017M on $62,286M). However, gross margin is materially affected by program accounting judgments and estimates (including accounting quantities, cost-to-complete and price escalation), which can change reported revenues and gross margins, so it is a less reliable standalone metric for Boeing.
The commercial airline industry worldwide and government and defense customers, including the U.S. government.
Yes — historically, the airline industry has been cyclical and very competitive and has experienced significant profit swings.
